title: Air Hockey Table — Centrifugal Blower + IR Goal Detector
design_question: How to build an Arduino-controlled air hockey table with automatic score detection?
selected_approach: 1200×600 mm MDF table, 2 mm holes at 25 mm spacing. 12 V centrifugal blower (100 W, 300 Pa static pressure, 1.0 m³/min) feeds plenum below table. Rail cushions: foam-backed ABS strips. Goal sensors: 2× IR photogate pairs (TCRT5000 emitter/receiver) at each goal slot (puck interrupts beam). Arduino Nano: counts goals, drives 7-segment scoreboard, plays buzzer on score. Two-player LED button interface for game start/reset.
rationale: Centrifugal blower provides uniform pressure to plenum box feeding all holes. IR photogate is reliable for 65 mm puck detection. TCRT5000 response < 1 ms ensures no missed goals. Arduino Nano is compact and sufficient for score logic.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Insufficient static pressure for plenum distribution.

verification_notes: FACT: 'Air Hockey Table — Puck Lift by Air Cushion and Rebound Dynamics' confirms required P_lift = 20 Pa and blower specifications.
supporting_fact_titles: Air Hockey Table — Puck Lift by Air Cushion and Rebound Dynamics
DC Motor Speed Control via PWM
